+package keyring
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"errors"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/zalando/go-keyring"
+)
+
+const timeout = 3 * time.Second
+
+var ErrNotFound = errors.New("password not found in keyring")
+
+func Set(ctx context.Context, service, user, password string) error {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	ch := make(chan error, 1)
+	go func() {
+		defer close(ch)
+		ch <- keyring.Set(hostname(service), user, password)
+	}()
+	select {
+	case err := <-ch:
+		return err
+	case <-ctx.Done():
+		return ctx.Err()
+	}
+}
+
+func Get(ctx context.Context, service, user string) (password string, err error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	type msg struct {
+		password string
+		err      error
+	}
+
+	ch := make(chan msg, 1)
+	go func() {
+		defer close(ch)
+		password, err := keyring.Get(hostname(service), user)
+		ch <- msg{password: password, err: err}
+	}()
+	select {
+	case res := <-ch:
+		if errors.Is(res.err, keyring.ErrNotFound) {
+			return "", ErrNotFound
+		}
+		return res.password, res.err
+	case <-ctx.Done():
+		return "", ctx.Err()
+	}
+}
+
+func hostname(service string) string {
+	return "cci:" + service

pr.author: jackieg017

comments.body:
### URL validation missing in non-interactive setup path **Medium Severity** The `ValidateHost` function is called in the interactive `setup` function (line 184) but not in `setupNoPrompt`. Users running `circleci setup --no-prompt --host INVALID_URL --token TOKEN` bypass URL validation entirely, allowing invalid URLs to be saved to config. This defeats the PR's purpose of providing clear error messages for invalid URLs during setup.
Additional Locations (1) - [`cmd/setup.go#L182-L187`](https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/blob/e9981a6257f8001853dede62753a190675a51bf7/cmd/setup.go#L182-L187)

pr.title: [WEBXP-28] Clear error message on invalid url
pr.author: hanabel1

comments.body:
### Missing `integrationTesting` check in `setupNoPrompt` validation **Medium Severity** The URL validation in `setupNoPrompt()` does not check `opts.integrationTesting` before calling `ValidateHost`, while the `setup()` function correctly wraps its validation with `if !opts.integrationTesting`. The PR description states validation is skipped during integration testing to avoid breaking tests with mock/invalid URLs, but this is only implemented in one of the two code paths. If integration tests use the `--no-prompt` flow with mock hosts from existing config, validation will fail unexpectedly.
Additional Locations (1) - [`cmd/setup.go#L182-L187`](https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/blob/c6af1fa0a8f57e6f63c37901b2e5b0be6338b88b/cmd/setup.go#L182-L187)

pr.title: [WEBXP-28] Clear error message on invalid url
pr.author: hanabel1

comments.body: ### URL validation in rest client is ineffective **Medium Severity** The URL validation check `baseURL == nil` in `NewFromConfig` is ineffective because `url.Parse` almost never returns a nil URL pointer. For example, `url.Parse("not-valid")` succeeds with `Host: ""`. The `ValidateHost` function correctly checks `parsedURL.Host == ""`, but this check is missing here. Users with invalid hosts from environment variables, command-line flags, or legacy config files will bypass the panic and later encounter confusing errors when API calls fail.

pr.title: [WEBXP-28] Clear error message on invalid url
pr.author: hanabel1

comments.body: ### TelemetrySettings may load and write to different paths **Low Severity** Unlike `Config` and `UpdateCheck` which store the file path in a `FileUsed` field during `Load()` and reuse it in `WriteToDisk()`, `TelemetrySettings` recomputes the path by calling `StatePath()` independently in both `Load()` and `Write()`. Since `StatePath()` dynamically checks whether the legacy file `~/.circleci/telemetry.yml` exists, if that file is created or deleted between `Load()` and `Write()` calls, settings could be loaded from one location (e.g., XDG path) but written to another (e.g., legacy path), causing data inconsistency.

pr.title: feat: add XDG Base Directory spec support for config files
pr.author: graybear-io
